title: Concurrent Rendering with Transitions
description:
Optimize a tab-based dashboard that freezes the UI during expensive tab switches by using React transitions and deferred values.

### Requirements

- Use `useTransition` for tab switching so the UI stays responsive
- Show a pending indicator while the transition is in progress
- The previous tab content remains visible (not replaced by a spinner) during the transition
- Use `useDeferredValue` for the search filter inside each tab panel
- The search input must remain responsive even when the filtered list is expensive
- Show a visual "stale" state (reduced opacity) while deferred content is outdated

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Tab button | `tab-{id}` |
| Active tab panel | `tab-panel` |
| Pending indicator | `pending` |
| Search input | `tab-search` |
| Results list | `results-list` |
| Stale indicator | `stale` |
constraints:
- Must use useTransition for tab switches
- Must use useDeferredValue for search deferral
- UI must not freeze during tab switch
- Previous tab content stays visible during transition
- Search input must remain responsive
hints: useTransition returns [isPending, startTransition]. Wrap the setActiveTab call in startTransition() so React treats it as low priority.
useDeferredValue(searchQuery) returns a value that lags behind. Compare it to searchQuery — if they differ, the content is 'stale'.
Keep the tab panel component with React.memo so it doesn't re-render unnecessarily during the transition.
